I’ve seen a lot of ORB strategies but the way you tie the 9:30 range to actual confirmation and then wait for the pullback makes it way more structured. The impulse strength deciding whether you enter at the top, mid, or bottom of the range actually makes sense instead of just blindly buying breakouts. I’ve been using the first light beacon with this kind of range + demand setup and it helps me see if order flow is really backing the breakout or not. When the structure, session timing, and liquidity all line up like that, the move feels a lot cleaner.

4:15 So you’re basically trading an Order Block. The issue here is price rejected from a previous high with an engulfing bearish candle, inverted the FVG at premium and created a bearish FVG, then inverted the FVG at discount, tapped the OB/demand zone and reacted to it BUT the green candle tapped the iFVG at premium with a HUGE wick, and the doji candle after it shows indecision. So how would you know if that demand zone is going to hold? At this point it could just range, but that rejection wick would make me lean a little bearish, unless there’s a HTF key level below you’re not showing.

This is probably the best video anyone can watch for an explanation to this strategy. Most other videos will just tell you that if a candle breaks above the range, you're looking long and if the candle breaks below the range, you're looking short.

Listen up please...You are the perfect strategy. Meaning, pick one like previous day hi and low. Mark them on your charts to carry over to the the next day. When price approaches the pdh go short if pdl go long. Set a 1:1 r r. Do this on a sim account. Keep everything simple. extract learning points or things you messed up write them down go back and try again. keep doing this until you have that strategy profitable. journal what u messed up on and refine over and over and over. do this with orb do this with anything and before long you have your strategy. take notes on entry but do not move the stop for at least 100 trades. Aftet 100 start managing the stop. Get to break even asap and see how that works. trail the stop and see how that works. the only way to be consistent is to design a strat that works for you and your psychology. Now.....get to work, there is money to be made out here and you are the perfect strategy
Not  be  jumping     from strategy  to strategy. That is whatgives  you some consistency.

Just started learning trading 2 months ago 6-10 hours a day I I was curious about this orb strategy I backtested my ORB (Opening Range Breakout) strategy on US500 for 5 months (Jan–May) and logged 96 trades with 76 wins and 20 losses (79.2% win rate) for +782 USD net (profit factor 3.46), and my biggest edge showed up around the 09:30 New York open, especially 10:00–10:45 ET.  I did my own price action changes to it and oh boy it works wonders mostly you’ll get rr 1 but can create a module for runners to rr 1:4 sometimes with this
